companies.name: Constellation Energy
companies.description: America's largest producer of clean, carbon-free energy, operating the nation's largest fleet of nuclear power plants across 12 states.

companies.name: Duke Energy
companies.description: One of the largest energy holding companies in the U.S., serving 8 million customers across the Southeast and Midwest with a fleet of nuclear, natural gas, and renewable assets.

companies.name: Dominion Energy
companies.description: A leading provider of electricity and natural gas, operating nuclear plants in Virginia and Connecticut and serving approximately 7 million customers across multiple states.

companies.name: Entergy
companies.description: An integrated energy company delivering electricity to 3 million customers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas, with a significant nuclear generation portfolio.

companies.name: NextEra Energy
companies.description: The world's largest producer of wind and solar energy, also operating nuclear plants through its subsidiary Florida Power & Light and serving over 12 million people.

companies.name: Tennessee Valley Authority
companies.description: A federally owned corporation and the nation's largest public power provider, operating three nuclear plants across the Tennessee Valley region serving 10 million people.

companies.name: Southern Nuclear
companies.description: Operator of Southern Company's nuclear fleet, including Plant Vogtle Units 3 and 4 — the first newly constructed reactors in the U.S. in over three decades — plus Hatch and Farley.

companies.name: Ameren Missouri
companies.description: A Missouri utility serving 1.2 million electric customers and operating the Callaway Energy Center, the state's only nuclear power plant.

companies.name: Energy Northwest
companies.description: A Washington State public power joint operating agency running the Columbia Generating Station, the Pacific Northwest's only commercial nuclear plant.

companies.name: Talen Energy
companies.description: An independent power producer operating the Susquehanna Steam Electric Station, a two-unit boiling water reactor plant in Pennsylvania.

companies.name: Arizona Public Service
companies.description: Arizona's largest electric utility and operator of the Palo Verde Generating Station, the largest nuclear power plant in the United States by output.

companies.name: Pacific Gas & Electric
companies.description: A California utility serving 16 million people and operating the Diablo Canyon Power Plant, the state's last operating nuclear facility.

companies.name: PSEG
companies.description: A New Jersey energy company whose PSEG Nuclear subsidiary operates the Salem and Hope Creek generating stations at the Artificial Island site.

companies.name: Xcel Energy
companies.description: A Midwest utility operating the Monticello and Prairie Island nuclear plants in Minnesota while leading a major clean-energy transition.

companies.name: DTE Energy
companies.description: A Michigan energy company operating the Fermi 2 nuclear plant on the shore of Lake Erie, serving 2.3 million electric customers.

companies.name: Indiana Michigan Power (AEP)
companies.description: An American Electric Power subsidiary operating the Donald C. Cook Nuclear Plant on Lake Michigan in southwest Michigan.

companies.name: Luminant (Vistra)
companies.description: A Texas-based power producer operating the Comanche Peak nuclear plant and, through the former Energy Harbor fleet, Davis-Besse, Perry, and Beaver Valley.

companies.name: STP Nuclear Operating Company
companies.description: Operator of the South Texas Project, a two-unit pressurized water reactor station southwest of Houston.

companies.name: Wolf Creek Nuclear Operating Corporation
companies.description: Operator of the Wolf Creek Generating Station near Burlington, Kansas, jointly owned by Evergy and Kansas Electric Power Cooperative.

companies.name: Nebraska Public Power District
companies.description: Nebraska's largest electric utility, operating the Cooper Nuclear Station on the Missouri River.

companies.name: Westinghouse Electric
companies.description: The world's leading nuclear energy company, supplying nuclear fuel, plant design, engineering and plant services to utilities worldwide. Headquartered in Cranberry Township, PA.

companies.name: Oklo
companies.description: An advanced fission company developing the Aurora powerhouse, a fast-reactor microreactor designed to deliver clean baseload power.

companies.name: TerraPower
companies.description: A nuclear innovation company founded by Bill Gates, building the Natrium sodium-cooled fast reactor and advancing medical isotope production.

companies.name: Kairos Power
companies.description: A developer of the fluoride salt-cooled, high-temperature reactor (KP-FHR), building the Hermes demonstration reactor in Oak Ridge, Tennessee.

companies.name: Urenco USA
companies.description: Operator of the only commercial uranium enrichment facility in the US, the National Enrichment Facility in Eunice, New Mexico.
